notes: Regular -y → -ies plural.
Less common derived adj. Teach as recognition only.
Regular plural.
To parent = to act as a parent. Less common. Recognition only.
Parental responsibility / parental leave. High collocation value.
Parenting = the activity of raising a child. Common in Task 2 essays.
Regular plural.
Generational gap / generational change. Recognition vocabulary.
Regular plural.
Base verb for marriage. teach separately as marry (v).
He/she marries.
She married him last year.
They are married (adj. use).
She is marrying next month.
Marital status / marital problems. Recognition vocabulary.
Regular plural.
Base verb. Relate to = to understand or connect with.
Related = connected. Polysemous (related to family / related to a topic). Review sense boundary.
Relation = connection or family member. Teach as separate headword later.
Regular plural.
Traditional values / traditional family. Very high frequency in IELTS essays.
Traditionally families did X. Common in Task 2 arguments.
Regular plural.
Regular plural. Note -y → -ies.
Responsible for something. High collocation value.
Act responsibly. Common in ethics and family contexts.
Comparative is more elderly not elderlier. Note to learners.
Financial independence / independence from parents. Core noun form.
Live independently. Common in elderly and youth contexts.
Teach as antonym pair. Dependent on family.
Emotional dependence / financial dependence. Teach after independence.
She supports her family.
He supported his parents financially.
She felt supported by her family.
She is supporting three children.
Support as noun is polysemous (emotional support / support beam / financial support). Teaching family/emotional sense here.
A supportive family. High collocation value.
She raises her children alone.
He was raised in Shanghai.
She was raised by her grandparents.
Raising children requires patience.
The influences on a child's development.
To influence someone. Same spelling as noun. Teach verb use separately.
An influential family member. Recognition vocabulary.
Regular plural. -y → -ies.
Regular plural.
Individual choice / individual responsibility. Same form as noun. Note dual use.
Assessed individually. Common in education and society contexts.
Sense of individuality. Higher-band recognition word.
Different cultures / across cultures.
Cultural differences / cultural values. Very high frequency in IELTS essays.
Culturally diverse. Common in Task 2 writing.
Family values / traditional values. Plural form very common in IELTS.
To value something = to consider it important. Same spelling. Polysemous. Teach verb sense later.
Valuable experience / valuable relationship. High frequency.
Attitudes toward family / attitudes are changing.
American plural. British: behaviours. Note American spelling policy.
To behave well / badly. Base verb for behavior.
She behaves differently at home.
The children behaved well.
American spelling: behavioral (British: behavioural). Behavioral problems / behavioral change.
Family conflicts / internal conflicts.
To conflict with something. Same spelling as noun. Stress shifts: CONflict (n.) vs conFLICT (v.). Human review for pronunciation.
Conflicting opinions / conflicting values. Common in Task 2 essays.
Feel conflicted = have mixed feelings. Useful for emotions context.
